freeze.title: Frost Risk – Immediate Action Required
freeze.message: Temperature is critically low and may cause frost damage to sensitive crops.
recommendedActions: Monitor crops overnight for frost damage

cold.title: Low Temperature Advisory
cold.message: Low temperature conditions may slow crop growth, especially for temperature-sensitive crops.

heat.title: Extreme Heat Stress Alert
heat.message: Very high temperature can cause heat stress and moisture loss in crops.
recommendedActions: Avoid fertilizer application during peak heat

warm.title: High Temperature Advisory
warm.message: Prolonged warm conditions may reduce soil moisture and affect crop productivity.
recommendedActions: Irrigate during early morning or evening

heavy-rain.title: Heavy Rainfall – Waterlogging Risk
heavy-rain.message: Heavy rainfall may lead to waterlogging and root damage.

rain.title: Rainfall Advisory
rain.message: Rainfall is generally beneficial for crops but prolonged moisture may increase disease risk.
recommendedActions: Observe crops for early disease symptoms

drought.title: Drought Stress Warning
drought.message: Low humidity combined with warm temperature increases the risk of moisture stress.
recommendedActions: Apply mulching to conserve soil moisture

wind.title: Strong Wind Alert
wind.message: Strong winds may cause physical damage or lodging in crops.

wind-moderate.title: Wind Advisory
wind-moderate.message: Moderate wind conditions may increase soil moisture loss.

snow.title: Snowfall Warning
snow.message: Snowfall may damage crops and block access routes.
recommendedActions: Protect crops from heavy snow accumulation
Clear snow from greenhouses and pathways

freeze-forecast.title: Forecasted Frost Risk
freeze-forecast.message: Temperatures are expected to drop significantly in the next 24 hours.

heavy-rain-forecast.title: Heavy Rainfall Forecast
heavy-rain-forecast.message: Heavy rainfall is expected soon, increasing the risk of waterlogging and soil erosion.
recommendedActions: Secure loose soil and field boundaries
